title: Matrix / canvas (strategy framework canvas) report
description: Data contract for the matrix-canvas format: a strategy whiteboard combining (1) a hero 2×2 plotted plane with labelled axes and positioned items, (2) a SWOT four-pane, and (3) a lean/business-model canvas grid of cells. The reader scans regions and item positions rather than reading prose. Renders to a self-contained, themeable, interactive HTML canvas board. The sample's #report-data is a valid instance of this schema.

meta.description: Shared report chrome (org / project / title / theme). Brand-neutral: use real or brand-neutral values (e.g. Independent Research / Open Research / Research Desk); never leave a {{...}} placeholder.

reportType.description: Eyebrow label shown above the canvas title, e.g. 'Strategy Workshop'.

title.description: Canvas title shown in the .canvas-head, e.g. 'SME Lending Launch — Strategy Canvas'.

subtitle.description: Optional short standfirst, e.g. 'Effort × Impact · SWOT · Lean Canvas — Q2 FY26 planning session'.

date.description: ISO date of the workshop / as-of date (YYYY-MM-DD).

theme.description: Initial brand theme; applied as <html data-theme>. Default for this format is 'indigo'.

matrix.description: The hero 2×2 plotted plane — the format's unmistakable silhouette. Contains axis definitions, quadrant labels, and the items plotted as positioned dots/cards.

axisX.description: The horizontal axis (left = low, right = high).

title.description: Axis title shown as the tracked-mono centre label, e.g. 'Effort'.

lowLabel.description: Label at the low (left) end of the axis, e.g. 'Low'.

highLabel.description: Label at the high (right) end of the axis, e.g. 'High'.

axisY.description: The vertical axis (bottom = low, top = high). Note: in CSS the Y coordinate is measured from the TOP; the schema uses natural 0=bottom / 100=top convention, and the renderer inverts to CSS.

title.description: Axis title shown rotated vertically, e.g. 'Impact'.

lowLabel.description: Label at the low (bottom) end, e.g. 'Low'.

highLabel.description: Label at the high (top) end, e.g. 'High'.

quadrants.description: The four quadrant labels in reading order: top-left, top-right, bottom-left, bottom-right.

label.description: Quadrant heading shown in the corner, e.g. 'Quick wins'.

sublabel.description: Optional sub-label under the heading, e.g. 'High impact, low effort'.

wash.description: Translucent background wash tone for this quadrant. 'ok' = green tint; 'hold' = amber tint; 'risk' = bordeaux tint; 'navy' = brand tint.

items.description: Items plotted as positioned dots or mini-cards inside the plane. Position is given as x (0=left, 100=right) and y (0=bottom, 100=top) percentages.

id.description: Short unique identifier shown as the dot number/label, e.g. '1' or 'A'.

label.description: Item name shown in the .plot-label chip, e.g. 'SME lending portal'.

x.description: Horizontal position as a percentage 0–100 (0 = left/low-effort, 100 = right/high-effort).

y.description: Vertical position as a percentage 0–100 (0 = bottom/low-impact, 100 = top/high-impact). The renderer converts to CSS top (100-y).

status.description: Dot/card colour state. 'ok' = proceed/strong (ok green); 'hold' = watch/medium (amber); 'risk' = defer/weak (bordeaux).

card.description: If true, render as a mini-card (.plot-item--card) with a left-border accent. If false (default), render as a bare dot + label chip.

meta.description: Optional one-line meta note shown inside the mini-card (.pc-meta), e.g. '~6 mo · £1.2M'.

flip.description: If true, the label chip appears to the left of the dot (flex-direction:row-reverse), useful when the dot is near the right edge.

swot.description: Optional SWOT four-pane below the hero matrix. Each pane is a short bullet list. Omit the swot object entirely if SWOT is not needed for this canvas.

subtitle.description: Optional frame subtitle / context, e.g. 'SME lending initiative'.

strengths.description: Strength bullet points (tinted ok-green). Each string is one bullet line.

canvas.description: Optional lean/business-model canvas grid of labelled cells, shown beside the SWOT. Omit if not needed.

subtitle.description: Optional frame context, e.g. 'SME lending launch'.

cells.description: The canvas cells laid out in a CSS grid (3 columns by default). Order determines visual placement left-to-right, top-to-bottom.

title.description: Cell label shown as the micro tracked-mono heading, e.g. 'Problem' or 'Unique value prop'.

span.description: Optional column span (CSS grid-column: span N). Defaults to 1. Use 2 or 3 for wide cells like 'Cost structure'.

rowSpan.description: Optional row span (CSS grid-row: span N). Defaults to 1.

accent.description: If true, this cell gets the brand-tinted accent wash (.canvas-cell.accent) to emphasise it, e.g. the value proposition cell.

items.description: Bullet points shown in the cell body, each a short terse string.

meta.title: SME Lending Launch — Strategy Canvas
meta.subtitle: Effort × Impact · SWOT · Lean Canvas — Q2 FY26 planning session
meta.author: Research Desk

swot.title: SWOT Analysis
swot.subtitle: SME lending initiative
strengths: Owned ledger + deposit base = cheaper funding cost
Existing SME depositor base as warm audience
Regulatory approval already in place for lending
weaknesses: No credit-decisioning IP — model must be built or bought
Legacy loan-origination system hard to extend
opportunities: SME lending under-served in two regions
Embedded-finance API as a new revenue line

cells.title: Unfair advantage
items: Owned ledger + deposit base = cheaper funding

cells.title: Cost structure
items: Model build & risk team · cloud compute · KYC vendor calls · loan-loss provision
